title: odontites vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Odontites vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as toothwort, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the borage family, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Odontites vulgaris subsp. vulgaris include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
